2606.09907.title: LongMoE: Longitudinal Multimodal Learning via Trajectory-Aware Mixture-of-Experts
2606.09907.paper_summary: Multimodal clinical learning is increasingly important for integrating diverse patient data, including imaging, text, and personalised health records. However, it faces two fundamental challenges: i) modality missingness, where arbitrary subsets of modalities are unavailable at a given patient visit, ii) longitudinal dynamics, where the diagnostic significance of an observation depends on the patient's evolving disease trajectory over time. Existing methods address these challenges in isolation: missing-modality frameworks treat each visit as an independent static snapshot and discard temporal context, while longitudinal models often assume complete modality availability and degrade under systematic modality incompleteness. We propose LongMoE (Longitudinal Mixture-of-Experts), the unified framework to jointly address both challenges. LongMoE combines a context-aware imputation module with an attentional tokenization module that captures frequency-domain temporal patterns across irregular visit sequences, a trajectory-aware encoder for modeling disease progression, and context-conditioned Sparse MoE routing for patient-specific expert selection. Experiments on ADNI, OASIS-3, and MIMIC-IV show that LongMoE improves robustness under missing or weak contemporaneous modalities and remains competitive in full-modality settings, establishing a strong foundation for longitudinally-aware multimodal clinical learning.
2606.09907.paper_summary_zh: 多模態臨床學習對於整合多樣化的患者數據（包括影像、文本和個性化健康記錄）越來越重要。<lb/>然而，它面臨兩個基本挑戰：i) 模態缺失，在特定的患者就診時，任意子集的模態不可用，ii) 縱向動態，觀察的診斷意義取決於患者隨時間演變的疾病軌跡。<lb/>現有方法孤立地解決這些挑戰：缺失模態框架將每次就診視為獨立的靜態快照，並丟棄時間上下文，而縱向模型則通常假設完整的模態可用，並在系統性模態不完整的情況下性能下降。<lb/>我們提出了LongMoE（縱向專家混合模型），這是一個統一框架，旨在共同解決這兩個挑戰。<lb/>LongMoE結合了上下文感知的插補模塊和一個注意力標記模塊，該模塊捕捉不規則就診序列中的頻域時間模式，還有一個考慮軌跡的編碼器用於建模疾病進展，以及基於上下文的稀疏MoE路由，用於患者特定的專家選擇。<lb/>在ADNI、OASIS-3和MIMIC-IV上的實驗表明，LongMoE在缺失或弱的當前模態下提高了穩健性，並在完整模態設置中保持競爭力，為縱向感知的多模態臨床學習奠定了堅實的基礎。
2606.09907.author: Maxx Richard Rahman et.al.
2606.09907.authors: Maxx Richard Rahman, Prakhar Kumar, Wolfgang Maass
